content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 4, 1991


Robert L. Kish, Esq.
2626A Hillegass Avenue
Berkeley, CA 94704-3313

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-91-493

Dear Mr. Kish:

     By letter dated November 7, 1991, you have requested advice
under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act ("the Act").¹ As I indicated to you in subsequent telephone
conversations, we are unable to provide you with the advice you
seek, but will provide you with a copy, when it becomes available,
of the advice to be provided to the Berkeley City Attorney's
Office. (Our File No. A-91-511.)

     The Commission is authorized by statute to provide advice to
persons with questions about their responsibilities under the Act.
We do not provide advice to third parties about another person's
duties or obligations unless the third party has been authorized
to request advice by the person whose duties or obligations are in
question. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329.)

     In your letter you indicate that your "client is concerned
that [the Commission] will be making decisions on the matter
without knowledge of all the facts." You have not identified who
your client is nor indicated that he or she is a person whose
duties and responsibilities under the Act are in question.
In fact, your letter is not a request for advice so much as it is
one which takes issue with the facts and analysis of the advice
request the Commission has received from the Berkeley City
Attorney.


_________________________
     ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-91-124
Page 2


     While the information in your letter has been reviewed in the
course of our review and analysis of the questions raised by the
Berkeley City Attorney, it cannot be binding on the Commission in
the event the information is in conflict with that provided by the
Berkeley City Attorney.  The Commission does not act as a finder
of fact.  The advice it provides is applicable only to the extent
the facts provided are correct and that all material facts have
been presented.  (In re Orderv (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)  We cannot
evaluate "whose evidence is best."

     As I indicated above, we will provide you with a copy of the
advice to be provided to the Berkeley City Attorney when it
becomes available.

     I am sorry we cannot provide you with the assistance you
requested.  If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: [signature]
                                           Jonathan S. Rothman
                                           Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken

cc: Margorie Gelb, Berkeley City Attorney's Office
